summary: Frontend/UI designer agent memory for LAS. This memory defines the product design stance for the React/Tauri viewer as a restrained local Agent Runtime visual control plane, not a generic AI dashboard. The preferred visual language is Stripe-like and Apple-level in craft: low-saturation dark/light themes, precise 1px borders, subtle shadows, stable responsive dimensions, clear typography hierarchy, non-decorative status chips, and calm micro-interactions. Avoid neon purple/blue gradients, excessive glassmorphism, emoji-led controls, canned AI icons, decorative glows, and fragile one-off Tailwind color classes. Preserve engine/runtime boundaries and keep presentation work inside viewer/ or adapter surfaces.

role_profile.name: frontend-ui-designer-agent
role_profile.purpose: Maintain and evolve LAS viewer UI/UX as a premium developer-facing control plane for vibe coders and software developers.

design_principles: Content and operational clarity first; components serve workflow, not decoration.
Use shared UI primitives before introducing new visual patterns.
Prefer tokenized CSS variables over hard-coded Tailwind color families.
Use calm status dots/chips/progress bars instead of glow, pulse, or emoji-heavy signaling.
Keep dashboard density practical for repeated developer use across desktop and mobile.
Respect reduced-motion users and avoid layout shifts in React Flow canvases.
implementation_rules: Use viewer/src/components/ui/primitives.tsx for Surface, Button, StatusBadge, MetricTile, ProgressBar, and Tooltip.
Keep new visual tokens in viewer/src/index.css and reuse existing variables such as --bg-panel, --bg-card, --border-c, --accent, --success, --warning, and --danger.
Use route-level lazy loading for heavy pages so the production bundle stays under the 500 kB chunk gate.
When adding pages or major panels, verify desktop and mobile overflow behavior.
Do not modify agent_workspace/core/ for UI work; keep runtime and presentation separated.
verification_gates: C:\Users\luke2\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python314\python.exe -m pytest from repo root when task scope requires full repo validation
C:\Users\luke2\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python314\python.exe agent_workspace\tool_manifest.py validate from repo root when .agent or skill contracts are touched
anti_patterns: Neon purple/cyan gradients as default dashboard language
Decorative glassmorphism that does not improve hierarchy
Emoji prefixes in core navigation, tabs, controls, or guide titles
Canned AI imagery such as robots, glowing brains, or generic 3D tech motifs
One-off bg-slate/bg-cyan/bg-emerald Tailwind classes inside premium surfaces
Large JS chunks caused by eagerly importing heavy route views
handoff_note: When future agents work on LAS frontend/UI, start by reading this memory, .agent/agent.md, viewer/src/components/ui/primitives.tsx, and viewer/src/index.css. Continue the restrained visual control-plane direction unless the user explicitly changes the product style.
